message:
refactor(runtime/undo): streamline command module imports and docs

Code quality improvements to the undo command module:

- Replace std::fmt::{Display, Formatter} with compact import
- Condense doc comments that were overly verbose
- Maintain all functionality and public API unchanged
- Improves code readability without altering behavior

These changes align with the project's style guidelines favoring
concise, clear code over excessive documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2","title":"Alien Artifact: Formal Resize Scheduler Model","description":"## Goal
Design a mathematically principled resize scheduler (control theory + change-point detection + anytime-valid testing).

## Scope
- Formal model of resize event stream with uncertainty.
- Decision rules for when to coalesce vs render immediately.
- Explicit failure modes and safeguards.

## Deliverables
- Model spec with assumptions/priors and decision thresholds.
- Pseudocode for scheduler + evidence ledger.

## Tests / Validation
- Property tests for decision rule invariants.
- Simulation harness for synthetic resize streams.
- E2E logs include scheduler decisions and confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scheduler is explainable and reproducible.
- Decision thresholds are defensible and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297481942Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297460572Z","close_reason":"Completed spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:24.807096454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien Artifact Math Notes (MANDATORY)

Purpose
- Provide a mathematically principled resize scheduler with anytime validity, explicit loss tradeoffs, and provable bounds on worst‑case behavior.

Core Model
- Treat resize events as a non‑stationary time series with regime shifts.
- Objective: minimize expected loss L(action, state) with asymmetric costs.

Loss Matrix (example)
- keep_reflow | skip_frame
  - smooth (true)  : 0   |  30
  - storm (burst)  : 10  |  1
- Intent: during bursts, skipping a frame is cheap; during steady state, skipping is costly.

Decision Rule (Bayes)
- Maintain posterior over regimes:
  P(storm|data_t) ∝ P(storm) × Π P(x_i|storm)
- Action a* = argmin_a Σ_c L(a,c) × P(c|data_t)

Anytime Validity (e‑process)
- Define e‑value e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Trigger “storm mode” when e_t ≥ 1/α (optional stopping safe).
- This lets the scheduler switch modes without invalidating guarantees.

Output Guarantees
- Bound expected latency under steady state.
- Bound average render cost under storms by throttling to max_fps.
- Explicitly log when bounds are violated.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","title":"Resize Scheduler: Change-Point Detection (BOCPD)","description":"## Task
Design a BOCPD-based regime detector for resize streams (burst vs steady states).

## Scope
- Define hazard function and run-length distribution.
- Output regime label + confidence for scheduler decisions.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests on synthetic streams (burst, periodic, noise).
- Simulation logs include detected change points and confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector is deterministic under fixed seed.
- Regime transitions are explainable and logged.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.343398831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:03:26.634191954Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:03:26.634171224Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.524512097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.343398831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BOCPD Details (Change‑Point Detection)

Model
- Observations: inter‑resize intervals Δt and magnitude |Δsize|.
- Assume piecewise‑stationary hazard λ (probability of regime change).
- Run length r_t tracks time since last change.

Recurrence
- p(r_t, x_t) = Σ_{r_{t-1}} p(r_{t-1}, x_{t-1}) × p(r_t|r_{t-1}) × p(x_t|r_{t-1})
- p(r_t=0|r_{t-1}) = λ (change)
- p(r_t=r_{t-1}+1|r_{t-1}) = 1-λ (no change)

Outputs
- Posterior P(r_t=0|x_1:t) gives change‑point probability.
- Use threshold τ_cp to enter storm mode.

Parameters
- λ chosen from empirical resize patterns (configurable, safe default).
- Likelihood modeled with exponential/gamma on Δt, mixture for |Δsize|.

Guarantees
- Sensitivity to burst onset; bounded false positives via λ and τ_cp.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:11Z"},{"id":233,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started. Waiting for docs/spec/resize-scheduler.md reservation to clear; plan to extend BOCPD section with concrete update equations, sufficient stats, truncation, and logging fields.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:01:56Z"},{"id":234,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added BOCPD implementation detail to docs/spec/resize-scheduler.md: sufficient stats + deterministic EMA updates, log-space run-length recursion, truncation/pruning strategy, and BOCPD evidence-ledger fields.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:03:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","title":"Resize Scheduler: Control-Theoretic Frame Pacing (PID)","description":"## Task
Define a PID/PI controller for frame pacing during resize storms.

## Scope
- Control signal based on resize velocity/acceleration.
- Tune parameters for stability and low latency.

## Tests / Validation
- Simulated resize streams with target stability metrics.
- Log controller outputs and overshoot metrics in JSONL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Controller avoids oscillation and minimizes lag.
- Parameters and rationale documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.526637079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:01:39.066516519Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:01:39.066499226Z","close_reason":"Completed PID spec section","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.707269019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.526637079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Control‑Theoretic Frame Pacing (PID/PI)

Goal
- Maintain target frame latency T (e.g., 16–33ms) under resize storms.

Signals
- error e_t = T - render_latency_t
- output u_t = throttle level (max FPS / skip ratio)

PID (or PI) Update
- u_t = Kp*e_t + Ki*Σ e + Kd*(e_t - e_{t-1})
- Clamp u_t to safe bounds [u_min, u_max]

Stability Notes
- Choose Kp, Ki from Ziegler‑Nichols‑style sweep on representative resize storms.
- Prefer PI (Kd=0) to avoid jitter from noisy latency measurements.

Fallback
- If measurements missing, default to conservative throttle.

Guarantee
- Bounded overshoot and monotone convergence to target latency (empirical).
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","title":"Resize Scheduler: Anytime-Valid Decision Rule (e-process)","description":"## Task
Define anytime-valid decision rules (e-process) for when to act under uncertainty in resize scheduling.

## Scope
- Specify e-process thresholds and stopping rules.
- Integrate with change-point detection and controller outputs.

## Tests / Validation
- Property tests: decisions remain valid under adversarial sequences.
- Simulation logs include evidence values and stop decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision rules are explainable and documented.
- Scheduler can justify actions in logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:17:13.088668519Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.888461745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Anytime‑Valid Decision Rule (e‑process)

E‑value Definition
- Construct e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Example: likelihood ratio between storm vs steady models.

Decision Rule
- Trigger storm mode if e_t ≥ 1/α (α = tolerated false‑positive rate).
- Safe under optional stopping (resize stream can end anytime).

Computation
- e_t = Π (p(x_t|storm) / p(x_t|steady)) with clipping to avoid underflow.
- Use log‑space accumulation for numerical stability.

Guarantee
- False alarm probability ≤ α regardless of stopping time.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","title":"Resize Scheduler: Anytime-Valid Decision Rule (e-process)","description":"## Task
Define anytime-valid decision rules (e-process) for when to act under uncertainty in resize scheduling.

## Scope

- Integrate with change-point detection and controller outputs.

- Simulation logs include evidence values and stop decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision rules are explainable and documented.
- Scheduler can justify actions in logs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:04:34.714168620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:14.888461745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Anytime‑Valid Decision Rule (e‑process)

E‑value Definition
- Construct e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Example: likelihood ratio between storm vs steady models.

Decision Rule
- Trigger storm mode if e_t ≥ 1/α (α = tolerated false‑positive rate).
- Safe under optional stopping (resize stream can end anytime).

Computation
- e_t = Π (p(x_t|storm) / p(x_t|steady)) with clipping to avoid underflow.
- Use log‑space accumulation for numerical stability.

Guarantee
- False alarm probability ≤ α regardless of stopping time.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","title":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger","description":"Proof sketches for invariants, plus explicit failure mode and mitigation ledger.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.888199761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:21.345399966Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:15.070056538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.888199761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger

Core Invariants
1. Continuous reflow: UI reflects latest size within SLA bound.
2. Atomic present: each frame is internally consistent (no partial clears).
3. No placeholder: real UI always rendered during resize.
4. No ghosting: shrinking clears stale regions deterministically.

Proof Sketches (outline)
- Atomic present: single buffered write + sync output ⇒ no interleaving.
- No ghosting: full clear of removed regions + reset scroll region ensures old cells removed.
- Continuous reflow: coalescer emits latest size each tick; scheduler bound ensures max delay.

Failure Mode Ledger
- Mux without sync output: fallback to overlay redraw + full clear.
- Extremely rapid resize: throttle via PI controller but never stalls input.
- Terminal caps mis-detection: conservative fallback and diagnostic log.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.20","title":"No-Flicker Proof Sketch + Counterexample Tests","description":"Formal proof sketch for no-flicker pipeline plus adversarial tests to surface counterexamples.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: No-Flicker Proof Sketch + Counterexample Tests
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:14.732274412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:14.023280667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:14.732274412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:29.580428126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:29.189113976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.20","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:29.390150027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.21","title":"Resize SLA Instrumentation + Alerts","description":"Instrument resize latency SLA and add alerting/logging when exceeded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Resize SLA Instrumentation + Alerts
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:19.855137322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:13.776702369Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:19.855137322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:29.965644670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.21","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:29.773819247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
